html:
COMMISSION IMPLEMENTING REGULATION (EU) 2026/1398

amending Implementing Regulation (EU) 2021/1378 as regards the recognition of certain control bodies as competent to carry out inspections and to issue organic certificates in third countries for the import of organic products into the Union, pursuant to Article 46 of the European Parliament and Council Regulation (EU) 2018/848.

THE EUROPEAN COMMISSION HAS ADOPTED THIS REGULATION:

Having regard to the Treaty on the Functioning of the European Union, Having regard to Regulation (EU) 2018/848 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 30 May 2018 on organic production and labelling of organic products and repealing Council Regulation (EC) No 834/2007 (1), in particular Article 46.1, and Whereas Annex II to Commission Implementing Regulation (EU) 2021/1378 (2) contains a list of control bodies recognized as competent to carry out inspections and to issue organic certificates in third countries for the import of organic products into the Union, pursuant to Article 46 of Regulation (EU) 2018/848, in accordance with Article 45.1(b)(i) thereof.

Article 46.2 of Regulation (EU) 2018/848 lays down the criteria that control bodies must meet in order to be recognized pursuant to Article 46.1 of that Regulation. Commission Delegated Regulation (EU) 2021/1698 (3) establishes the procedural requirements that these control bodies must comply with when submitting their application for recognition, in the form of technical documentation, to the Commission.

The Commission, in accordance with Article 1 of Delegated Regulation (EU) 2021/1698, has received and reviewed recognition requests from the following certification bodies: Baltic Testing India Private Limited, KAYOS ULUSLARARASI SERTİFİKASYON VE DENETİM HİZMETLERİ LİMİTED ŞİRKETİ, LETIS AUDIT & CERTIFICATION SA, MS Agroland Services Private Limited, VCERT QUALITY CERTIFICATION PRIVATE LIMITED, and ZENITH CERTIFICATIONS PRIVATE LIMITED. Based on the information in the technical documentation, the Commission has concluded that it is justified to recognize these certification bodies indefinitely for certain product categories in specific third countries.

The Commission has also received and reviewed requests from the following certification bodies, in accordance with Article 2 of Delegated Regulation (EU) 2021/1698: bio.inspecta AG, Control Union Certifications B.V., Florida Certified Organic Growers and Consumers, Inc. (FOG), DBA as Quality Certification Services (QCS), IBD Certificações Ltda, IMOcert Latinoamérica Ltda, IMO Control Private Limited, INDOCERT, Lacon Quality Certification Private Limited, ONECERT INTERNATIONAL PRIVATE LIMITED, and Organizacion Internacional Agropecuaria SA, to expand the scope of their recognition to certain product categories and specific third countries. Based on the information in the technical documentation, the Commission has concluded that it is justified to recognize these certification bodies indefinitely for the product categories and third countries covered by the requests.

The Commission has received requests from Bio Latina Certificadora and Southern Cross Certified Australia Pty Ltd to limit the scope of recognition by removing certain third countries, certain product categories, or both. Therefore, the scope of recognition for these certification bodies should be updated accordingly.

Implementing Regulation (EU) 2021/1378 should therefore be amended in this regard.

The measures prescribed in this regulation are consistent with the opinion of the committee for organic production.

Annex II to Implementing Regulation (EU) 2021/1378 shall be amended in accordance with the annex to this regulation.

This regulation enters into force on the third day after its publication in the Official Journal of the European Union.

This regulation is binding in its entirety and directly applicable in all Member States.

(1) OJ L 150, 14.6.2018, p. 1, ELI: http://data.europa.eu/eli/reg/2018/848/oj.

(2) Commission Implementing Regulation (EU) 2021/1378 of August 19, 2021, establishing certain rules for the certificate issued to operators, groups of operators and exporters in third countries participating in the import of organic products and products undergoing conversion into the Union, and establishing the list of recognized control bodies and control organizations pursuant to Regulation (EU) 2018/848 of the European Parliament and of the Council (OJ L 297, 20.8.2021, p. 24, ELI: http://data.europa.eu/eli/reg_impl/2021/1378/oj).

(3) Commission Delegated Regulation (EU) 2021/1698 of July 13, 2021, supplementing Regulation (EU) 2018/848 of the European Parliament and of the Council with regard to procedural requirements for the recognition of control bodies and control organizations authorized to carry out checks on organic operators and groups of operators and on organic products in third countries, as well as with rules on their supervision and the controls and other measures that these control bodies and control organizations shall perform (OJ L 336, 23.9.2021, p. 7, ELI: http://data.europa.eu/eli/reg_del/2021/1698/oj).

In Annex II to Implementing Regulation (EU) 2021/1378, the third paragraph shall be amended as follows:

After the entry for Agricert – Certificação de Produtos Alimentares Lda, the following entry shall be inserted:

Relevant code numbers, third countries and product categories:

The entry for Bio Latina Certificadora shall be replaced with the following:

Affected product codes, third countries, and product categories:

The entry for bio.inspecta AG will be replaced with the following:

Affected product codes, third countries, and product categories:

( *1 ) This designation does not affect positions on Kosovo's status and is in accordance with UN Security Council Resolution 1244/1999 and the International Court of Justice's advisory opinion on Kosovo's declaration of independence.

The entry for Control Union Certifications B.V. will be replaced with the following:

Affected product codes, third countries, and product categories:

( *2 ) Products originating from territories occupied by Israel since June 1967 shall not be certified as originating in Israel.

( *3 ) This designation does not affect positions on Kosovo's status and is in accordance with UN Security Council Resolution 1244/1999 and the International Court of Justice's advisory opinion on Kosovo's declaration of independence.

Exemptions: Products within product categories marked with "°" that are covered by a trade agreement for organic products, pursuant to Article 47 of Regulation (EU) 2018/848, or by the scope of recognition as equivalent of the third country listed in Annex I to Implementing Regulation (EU) 2021/2325.

The entry for Florida Certified Organic Growers and Consumers, Inc. (FOG), DBA as Quality Certification Services (QCS) will be replaced with the following:

"Florida Certified Organic Growers and Consumers, Inc. (FOG), DBA as Quality Certification Services (QCS)

tldr: The European Union has updated the list of organizations authorized to inspect and certify organic products originating from countries outside the EU. This clarifies which companies are permitted to issue certifications for products that can be sold as organic within Europe.
lattlast:
The European Commission has revised the regulations governing how certain control bodies can issue organic certificates. This means that organizations responsible for verifying that food and other products are organic before they are shipped to the EU have had their authorizations updated or expanded.

Some companies have been approved to inspect a wider range of products in more countries outside the EU. Other organizations have modified their procedures, now covering fewer aspects than previously. The goal is to ensure that organic products sold in Europe truly comply with the regulations governing organic production.

This change involves updating the list of approved control bodies, including specific names and details about which countries and product categories they are authorized to work with. This makes it easier for both importers and authorities to determine which certificates are valid when goods are imported into EU member states.
